macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto futures see $134 million in liquidations over 24 hours
items.summary: The cryptocurrency derivatives market saw roughly $134 million in liquidations over the past day, with Bitcoin and Ethereum leading the wipeout. The surge reflects elevated volatility across major tokens, including smaller liquidations in Solana and XRP futures.

items.title: Crypto market sees $388 million in long liquidations in 24 hours
items.summary: A separate market report says more than $388 million in long positions were liquidated in the last 24 hours as traders were hit by sharp price swings. Analysts cited macro uncertainty and fragile market conditions as key drivers of the move.

items.title: Bitcoin and major altcoins drop sharply amid market volatility
items.summary: Market commentary indicates Bitcoin fell nearly 3% to around $79,080, while Ethereum dropped more than 3% to about $2,219. The broader selloff highlighted pressure across major crypto assets as volatility increased.

items.title: Binance market update shows crypto market cap still above $2.6 trillion
items.summary: Binance’s latest market update says the global cryptocurrency market capitalization stands around $2.68 trillion, up 0.76% over the prior day. The update suggests that despite recent liquidations and price swings, the broader market remains elevated.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: GTA 6 pre-order rumors circulate, but no official confirmation from Rockstar
items.summary: Gaming creators are reporting leaked emails and retailer chatter suggesting Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders could open as soon as Monday, May 18. However, Best Buy has said the game is not yet available for pre-order and Rockstar has not announced an official date.

items.title: PlayStation 5 beta adds Welcome Hub widget showing top-played games by country
items.summary: A new PS5 Welcome Hub widget is reportedly in beta and displays the most-played games in a user’s country, along with weekly player counts for the top 10 titles. The feature is expected to expand social visibility around play habits.

items.title: Moss: The Forgotten Relic announced for multiple platforms including Switch 2 and PC
items.summary: Gematsu reports that Moss: The Forgotten Relic has been announced for PS5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, Switch, and PC. The new entry continues the Moss series and is slated for release this summer.

items.title: Pocketpair files Palworld Online trademark
items.summary: A new trademark filing from Pocketpair has sparked speculation about a multiplayer or service-based expansion for Palworld. The filing does not confirm a game announcement, but it suggests the company may be exploring related branding.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple reportedly testing Intel's 18A-P process for future iPhone and Mac chips
items.summary: Apple is said to be evaluating Intel's advanced 18A-P manufacturing process for a future run of chips, with the initial focus reportedly on older Apple Silicon systems. The move could signal a broader diversification of Apple’s chip supply chain.

items.title: Meta begins bringing third-party apps and games to its display glasses
items.summary: Meta is expanding the capabilities of its $800 smart glasses by opening them up to third-party apps and games. The update could make the glasses a much more practical mixed-reality platform.

items.title: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon team up to reduce mobile coverage dead zones
items.summary: The three major U.S. carriers are pooling resources to improve satellite capacity and expand coverage in underserved areas. The collaboration marks a rare industry-wide effort to tackle connectivity gaps.

items.title: Microsoft reportedly fixing a long-standing annoyance in Windows 11
items.summary: A new Windows 11 update appears to address one of the operating system's most persistent user complaints. The change is part of a broader stream of refinements to the Windows desktop experience.

items.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT personal finance tools
items.summary: OpenAI has introduced personal finance features for ChatGPT Pro users in the U.S., including support for connecting bank accounts through Plaid. The tool is designed to help users analyze spending and manage finances more easily.

items.title: Semiconductor stocks fall after Trump-Xi summit ends without major chip deal
items.summary: Global chip stocks dropped sharply after the Trump-Xi summit concluded without significant semiconductor agreements. Nvidia and AMD were among the biggest decliners as uncertainty over China-related chip sales persisted.

items.title: xAI launches Grok Build in early beta
items.summary: xAI has released Grok Build, an agent and command-line tool for coding, app building, and workflow automation. The beta is initially available to SuperGrok Heavy subscribers.

items.title: Red Hat opens Ansible to AI agents with new controls
items.summary: Red Hat has made its Ansible MCP server generally available and is previewing an orchestrator that channels AI-driven actions through deterministic playbooks. The company says the goal is to keep automation flexible while maintaining control.

items.title: AWS hit by US-East-1 outage after data center thermal event
items.summary: Amazon Web Services suffered an outage in its US-East-1 region after a cooling system failure caused servers to overheat and shut down. The incident affected core services including EC2 and EBS and highlighted the concentration risk of a heavily used cloud region.
